probability.last_reasoning: Bear case increased from 10% to 12%: compound stress acceleration (JGB +9.5bps in 2 days, all three legs converging on thresholds), Senate crypto advocate departures weakening legislative path, and persistent ODL data gap. Base case reduced from 40% to 42% to absorb RLUSD recovery. Mid case increased from 38% to 34% — actually decreased slightly because macro deterioration is accelerating faster than institutional adoption metrics are improving. The legislative risk from departing senators and the accelerating JGB trajectory justify shifting 4% from mid to bear/base. Bull case unchanged at 12% — no new bull catalysts this cycle.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Score elevated from baseline due to: persistent ODL data blackout (most critical thesis metric is invisible), Senate crypto advocate departures weakening legislative path, 13 days of zero permissioned DEX adoption, and accelerating macro stress convergence. The thesis infrastructure narrative is building but measurable adoption metrics remain critically absent.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: SWIFT continues to serve 11,500+ banks with incremental upgrades; no evidence of XRPL displacing any SWIFT corridor in production.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Visa B2B Connect operates but has not scaled aggressively into cross-border settlement; not a direct competitive threat currently.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Kinexys (formerly Onyx) processes $2B+ daily for JPMorgan clients with institutional-grade settlement — a live, scaled competitor in the exact market XRPL targets.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: BIS Nexus represents central bank-backed multilateral settlement infrastructure explicitly designed to eliminate the need for private bridge currencies like XRP — the most credible existential threat to the thesis.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: BlackRock's BUIDL fund, growing institutional DeFi on Ethereum L2s, and broader RWA tokenization on Ethereum maintain competitive pressure on XRPL's institutional positioning.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume data has been dark for extended periods — without verifiable throughput data, the core utility thesis (XRP as settlement bridge) cannot be confirmed or denied, which itself is bearish given Ripple's historical willingness to publish positive metrics.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: No hold-time or velocity data available; if XRP is used purely as a transient bridge with sub-second hold times and no accumulation incentive, price appreciation from utility alone may be structurally limited regardless of volume.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: KOSPI -20% in 2 days, Fear & Greed at 22 (Extreme Fear), Asian market instability, and compound stress convergence signal elevated recession probability that would suppress institutional risk appetite for crypto adoption.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: XRP at $1.43 is well off highs, Extreme Fear dominates, but broader crypto structure hasn't broken — Korean retail rotating in and 7 live XRP ETFs provide floor support.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Departure of Lummis, Daines, and potentially Blackburn from Senate removes key crypto advocates; while SEC guidance is constructive, legislative momentum could stall without replacements of comparable conviction.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 4.06% with BOJ March meeting live for another hike — divergent central bank policies could extend restrictive conditions and suppress institutional allocation to risk assets.

bear_case.bear_narrative: HOLD. Three kill switches at WARNING but none triggered. The thesis stands on institutional evidence — 7 ETFs with $1.4B cumulative inflows, SBI expanding during yen stress, Franklin Templeton publicly endorsing the settlement layer narrative — but its measurability is degrading dangerously. The ODL data vacuum is the single largest risk: a settlement thesis you cannot verify is a thesis running on faith, and faith has a shelf life. Compound stress is pre-loaded with all three legs converging on elevated thresholds; one exogenous shock could cascade. The paradox holds: the same macro stress that threatens XRP's price validates the structural need for the infrastructure it represents. Bear pressure at 34 — moderate, manageable, but rising on the measurement axis. Watch Q2 ETF filings for any volume disclosure. That is the next decisive data point.
